6. Graph
Due to time limit, Graph only covered some basic concepts and didn’t appear in the final examination this semester.

Difficulty: 数竞一试, some basic mathematics Olympic questions. If you learned Olympics before, you’ll certainly have no problem.
comment_teaching:
Frankly speaking I don’t know quite well. I attend the course for 2 or 3 lectures and I find Arya’s teaching is quite well-structured.
He is responsible that he conducted a make-up lecture via zoom.
But I couldn’t say more as I really didn’t know…
comment_grading:
Quite Lenient:
Midterm 40%, Final 50%, Assignment 10%

Midterm Mean: 80.5/100
Mine: 88/100
Final Mean: 79.7/100
Mine: 98/100

Overall: ~94.2  Final Grade: A+
Assignment nearly all the full marks.
Be well-prepared for the exam you will have no problem. Just remember to refer to the textbook and review more combinatorial math (since it’s more difficult).
comment_workload:
Light.
Only study 1 hour to see the slides and spend 1-2 hours on each assignment. And review 6 hours for the midterm and 3 hours for final examination.
If you have some Olympics foundation, I think you will do a great job with a little effort.

author: Rigbones
title: I love COMP 2711H !!!
comment_content:
Logic
Basic number theory
Counting
Probability
Induction, recursion
Graphs (briefly)
comment_teaching: Sunil's teaching is top notch (just like desmond!!!).  He stresses intuition and understanding over memorization which is really great.  He also enjoys teaching a lot which can be seen by his excited voice when he talks about certain theorems lol.  This semester, classes were at 9am but I attended every single one just because of Sunil.
comment_grading:
Really, really good grade.  Mean is about A-, which means around half of the class can get A range!!

Grades for reference:
Midterm  100/100,      mean 81.76
HW1  19/20,               mean 18.4
HW2  20/20,               mean 17.95
HW3  20/20,               mean 18.39
HW4  20/20,               mean 17.34
Finals  95//100,          mean 83.9
Letter grade:  A+

I know someone who got slightly below mean on midterm and mean in final and got A-,  really really good grade
comment_workload:
Moderate workload.  

HW problems are really hard and some of them can take you 2, 3 days to think about it yourself, but the problems are all standard problems so you can always google the answer if you don't know.  

If you can do all the HW problems without googling, you probably can get an A+.

author: wlph17

author: Lucien▪Evans
title: a good grading course with excellent teaching
comment_content:
This term Sunil covered Logic, Number theory, Counting, Probability, Induction and Recursion, graphs.

Probably due to the typhoon, he didn't finish the part of graphs, and only briefly introduced this part.
comment_teaching:
Following his explanation in discrete mathematics, you will understand it easily since Sunil will explain all these theories in detail and give some examples.

If there is a ranking in all the courses i have taken, i will position this course in top 3.

The only problem is that it might take you some time to get familiar with his accent.

Sunil didn’t make his course hard to understand. I didn’t have the background in discrete mathematics but didn't take too much time to follow with his lecture.
comment_grading:
Final and midterm are very easy and mainly based on the HW and tutorial. As long as you finish these two and write the past paper, you will find every problem is very familiar.

Just for reference:
midterm: mean 82.54/100
HW1: 18.42/20
HW2: 17.94/20
HW3: 18.41/20
HW4: 17.36/20

My score:
midterm: 100/100
HW1:19/20
HW2,3,4: 20/20

I am waiting for the final scores and will give a supplement after receiving the final results.

Final: 92/100

Well, my final is not that good since I didn't read the problem very well. It asked us to prove an identity with the  inductive tools but I prove it directly. This my scores in this problem were almost deducted.

I thought I can only got a A but eventually got an A+. Sunil, I like you!

Just for reference, my overall score is 95 very approaching 96. Still don't know the letter grading scheme.

author: Abraham.H
title: An excellent course as an introduction to discrete math for CS
comment_content:
The content of this course is very comprehensive to me. It covers a great range of topics like Logic, Number theory, Probability, Recursion, etc.
It can be a bit tricky for beginners like me. I've put quite a lot of effort into this course to understand those concepts and theories, but I have to say I'm fully satisfied with this course's content.
comment_teaching: Prof. Sunil's teaching method is great. He will guide you to think about how the problem comes out and how we develop a solution to it step by step.
comment_grading:
I heard that overall the grading scheme is very good.

My grades:
5 assignments: 19/20, 20/20, 19/20, 20/20, -/- (the last one was not marked)
midterm: 100/100
final: 100/100
Got A+
comment_workload:
5 assignments, each with 8-10 questions
midterm + final

Almost all the questions in assignments and exams are proof questions, which requires you to memorize those theories and use them in the right way. They are not unreasonably difficult to do, as long as you are familiar with those several question patterns. But it did take me quite a long time to memorize theories and practice proving.
I have to spend at least 5 hours on each assignment. TA also provides supplementary questions of each chapter for revision.
Exam questions are not as tricky as assignment questions.

author: APKB
title: This is a semi-rigorous proof-based mathematics course, quit if you were not prepared to go MATH1023.
comment_content: All are proofs. If you are not good at logical proof, don't come. Go 2711 instead as the grading is shit.
comment_teaching:
His accent is terrible and I don't know why now 2024 still have CS prof only use whiteboard and markers. He didn't teach how to find clues to prove the logic. Just metaphor, lecture doing 1 + 1 while exam doing prove 1 + 1 = 2.

Henry (UGTA) is good :)
comment_grading:
Background:
DSE Math+M2 5**, DSE ICT (database) 5*, COMP1021 A+, COMP2012H A.

Slightly above mean score, B+, quite fair tho, but I expected an A- for my effort. aka, A range < 50%, which is bad for an honor course. At least grading is worse than 2012H Quan Long.

Exam was not hard but if they think your proofs are not good, they won't give you mark even though you think you proved something.
comment_workload: I did a lot of question bank and tutorial questions for preparing the exam and wasted me a lot of time. I attended all lectures and completed weekly tutorial homework which is not compulsory. Homework could be time-consuming if you don't have resources.
